stem: A proposal enters the grant application pipeline. The probability of passing departmental review is between 61% and 73% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing external peer review is 71%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing panel discussion is 94%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing funding decision is 96%. What is the overall probability that the proposal is funded?

ground_truth.answer: P(funded) ranges from 0.391 to 0.468 as the departmental review pass rate varies from 61% to 73%. Point estimate: 0.429.

solution_steps: The proposal must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the departmental review stage creates a 7.7 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the departmental review rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
